design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: MPDAG total-effect invariance witness for P(Y | do(A)). Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are IDA for CPDAG effect multisets, Perkovic-Kalisch-Maathuis generalized adjustment in CPDAGs/PAGs, and amenability criteria for identifying total effects from partially oriented graphs. Why non-trivial? The target is not CPDAG enumeration or reuse of the generalized adjustment criterion: require a minimal partially directed graph witness class with two Markov-equivalent DAG completions sharing the same observational factorization and the same available adjustment variables, but inducing different total effects; then prove an exact orientation-cut certificate characterizing when the total-effect functional is invariant over all DAG completions. Why promising? The nonroutine object is a graph certificate: a hand-checkable ambiguous-first-edge cut on possibly causal A-to-Y paths, plus a paired linear-Gaussian witness law showing non-invariance when the cut fails. If the delta reduces to IDA enumeration, amenability definition unfolding, support coverage, or standard adjustment-set existence, pivot or accept field-tier.

banked_reason: Stage -1.1 rejected the topic as already solved: Perkovic 2020 already gives the necessary-and-sufficient MPDAG total-effect identification/invariance criterion, including the undirected first-edge failure condition and sufficiency theorem.

seeds_burned.one_liner: (seed index 0 — one-liner not found in state.json)
seeds_burned.reason: Already solved by Perkovic 2020; do not retry pure MPDAG/CPDAG orientation certificates as flagship thmsmith topics.
